title: NIST CSF Maturity Posture Dashboard

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST CSF ID.AM-01 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF ID.AM-01 (Asset inventory) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.7.1: NIST CSF Maturity Posture Dashboard.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.7.1' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `| rest /services/saved/searches` (title, disabled, eai:acl.app); `| from datamodel Risk.All_Risk` (search_name, risk_score, _time); CSV lookup `nist_csf_es_function_mapping` (correlation_search_name, nist_csf_function)
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), CIM Risk data model
spl:
| rest /services/saved/searches splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0 eai:acl.app="SplunkEnterpriseSecuritySuite" title="*Correlation Search*"
| lookup nist_csf_es_function_mapping correlation_search_name AS title OUTPUT nist_csf_function
| stats count as enabled_detections by nist_csf_function
```
```spl
| from datamodel Risk.All_Risk
| timechart span=7d sum(risk_score) as weekly_risk_points, dc(search_name) as distinct_risk_rules
description: Maps enabled ES correlation searches and risk scoring volume to NIST CSF functions for a defensible, data-driven maturity snapshot rather than a static policy diagram.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Maturity Posture Dashboard. The NIST Cybersecurity Framework tells us what good practice looks like, and we keep evidence we match it.
implementation: (1) Create `nist_csf_es_function_mapping.csv` with `correlation_search_name` = full saved-search title and `nist_csf_function` in {Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover}; (2) adjust `eai:acl.app` if your ES app name differs; (3) refresh the REST panel after content upgrades; (4) document CSF tier targets separately in narrative.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), CIM Risk data model.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `| rest /services/saved/searches` (title, disabled, eai:acl.app); `| from datamodel Risk.All_Risk` (search_name, risk_score, _time); CSV lookup `nist_csf_es_function_mapping` (correlation_search_name, nist_csf_function).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Create `nist_csf_es_function_mapping.csv` with `correlation_search_name` = full saved-search title and `nist_csf_function` in {Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover}; (2) adjust `eai:acl.app` if your ES app name differs; (3) refresh the REST panel after content upgrades; (4) document CSF tier targets separately in narrative.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| from datamodel Risk.All_Risk
| timechart span=7d sum(risk_score) as weekly_risk_points, dc(search_name) as distinct_risk_rules
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIST CSF Maturity Posture Dashboard (Identify/Protect/Detect/Respond/Recover)** — Maps enabled ES correlation searches and risk scoring volume to NIST CSF functions for a defensible, data-driven maturity snapshot rather than a static policy diagram.

Documented **Data sources**: `| rest /services/saved/searches` (title, disabled, eai:acl.app); `| from datamodel Risk.All_Risk` (search_name, risk_score, _time); CSV lookup `nist_csf_es_function_mapping` (correlation_search_name, nist_csf_function). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), CIM Risk data model. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `from` (dataset / Federated Search) — verify dataset availability and permissions.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=7d** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with ES Incident Review for the relevant correlation. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (enabled_detections by CSF function), Area chart (weekly_risk_points), Table (raw mapping for assessors).
visualization: Bar chart (enabled_detections by CSF function), Area chart (weekly_risk_points), Table (raw mapping for assessors).
knownFalsePositives: Identification gaps during new asset onboarding, M&A integration, or after refresh waves.
